messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
First collision: A and B meet at \(t = \frac{40-10}{3+1} = 7.5\) s at position \(32.5\) cm.  
After bouncing, A moves left at 3 cm/s, B moves right at 1 cm/s, and C continues left at 2 cm/s.

A reaches the left end at \(t = 7.5 + \frac{32.5}{3} = \frac{55}{3}\) s.  
At that same time, B and C meet: B (right-moving at 1) and C (left-moving at 2) meet at \(32.5 + 1\cdot\frac{65}{6} = \frac{130}{3}\) cm.  
After this bounce, B moves left at 1 cm/s and C moves right at 2 cm/s.

C then reaches the right end at \(t = \frac{55}{3} + \frac{100 - 130/3}{2} = \frac{140}{3}\) s.  
B reaches the left end at \(t = \frac{55}{3} + \frac{130/3}{1} = \frac{185}{3}\) s.

Thus the last ant falls off at \(\frac{185}{3}\) seconds.

ANSWER: 185/3
